content: Factor investing targets specific return drivers: value (cheap stocks), size (small caps), momentum (recent winners), quality (profitable, low debt), and low volatility (defensive stocks). Factors explain most return variation across portfolios per Fama-French research. Implementation via smart beta ETFs (rules-based, transparent) or active managers. Benefits: targeted exposure, diversification, lower fees than active management. Challenges: factor cyclicality (value underperformed growth for decade post-2010), crowding (too much capital chasing same factor), and data mining risk (spurious factors). Multi-factor approaches reduce single-factor timing risk. Factor timing is difficult—research suggests static allocations outperform attempts to rotate based on valuations or macro conditions.
